note: Discoverable cross-domain combination recipes. Each braids TWO abilities from different domains into a third effect neither reaches alone (skill_functions.json doctrine). Extends the SNG-010 emergence-recipe engine. DISCOVERABILITY: recipes are found by USE (pairing the two abilities in a beat surfaces the braid as a discovery, logged to the codex + narrativeLog), by a rune-cast/Attention read that hints a latent braid, or by a teacher who knows it. `discovery` field says how each is found. Owning both parts is the gate (class-cost/capacity prices the breadth; this is the payoff). Authored by Aevi (PO) — a starter set; generative braids (SNG-020) extend it in-grain.

recipes.name: The Warding Peace
recipes.effect: A light-bound truce-field: violence cannot be RAISED within it, not merely blocked. The cause of harm is forbidden, lit and held.
recipes.cannot: Holds only while sustained; a will already committed to violence strains it; does not stop harm from outside the field.
recipes.discovery: use — shield + de-escalate together in one beat; or taught at the Marchward

recipes.name: The Unseen Cut
recipes.effect: A decisive strike from a direction that was never there — the disciplined kill delivered through concealment. Violence carried on shadow.
recipes.cannot: Total light defeats the concealment; one strike, then you're seen; the Edge's honor-forms frown on it (a Redline reputation cost).
recipes.discovery: use — strike from concealment; or an Umbral assassin's teaching

recipes.name: The Mercy-Knowing
recipes.effect: The holder of both life and death can KNOW, truly, whether a body should be pulled back or let go — and do either rightly. The Quiet-in-the-Quickwood as a skill.
recipes.cannot: Knowing is not commanding the outcome the world allows; the most sacred and most terrible braid — it will not lie to you about which mercy is called for.
recipes.discovery: use — hold both traditions and attend a dying thing; the braid surfaces itself. The rarest teaching, held by few.
recipes.note: Same-domain poles (life+death) rather than two domains — the co-firing braid; the exception that proves combination is about POLES not just packs.

recipes.name: The Meaning-Engine
recipes.effect: A machine commanded by WHY, not just HOW — a Precursor work run by significance. The only way to truly treat with What Sleeps Under, which is both mechanism and meaning.
recipes.cannot: Requires dense substrate; the machine still obeys its physics, only now also its meaning; What Sleeps Under may answer, and answering is its own peril.
recipes.discovery: use at a deep Precursor site holding both; or Ledda / the Deep Warden may hint it
recipes.note: The braid the deep arc is built around.

recipes.name: The Riding Order
recipes.effect: Unmake a structure and re-bind its pieces mid-collapse into what you need — chaos and order in one motion. The Living-Lattice jewel as a technique.
recipes.cannot: The window is a heartbeat; miss the re-bind and you've only made chaos; drifts both poles at once, exhausting.
recipes.discovery: use — break and bind in the same beat; learned at the Living Lattice

recipes.name: The Whole Act
recipes.effect: Body and mind as one instrument — thought and motion stop being separate; you act faster than either could decide. The Middle Way's ideal made a skill.
recipes.cannot: Both must be trained near-equally; a body-heavy or mind-heavy wielder gets a lesser version; the extremes (Temple, Cogitarium) cannot do it at all.
recipes.discovery: use — hold both near-equal and act under pressure; taught at the Middle Way

recipes.name: The Sounding
recipes.effect: Ring a place and READ what rings back — map a location's whole pole-disposition and hidden co-firings at once. Resonance as sonar for the coordinate-world.
recipes.cannot: Loud — announces you; reveals disposition, not intent; a dead-silent (origin-flat) place returns nothing.
recipes.discovery: use — resonate then perceive; a Heights rune-reader's trick

recipes.name: The Harbored Flame
recipes.effect: A hidden sanctuary of restoring light — conceal a place so well it can't be found, and fill it with healing radiance the dark would otherwise forbid. Shelter AND mending in one.
recipes.cannot: The two poles fight — sustaining both is costly; a searcher who brings their own light can crack it.
recipes.discovery: use — conceal + heal a hidden space; an Umbral healer's craft

recipes.name: The Rooted Stand
recipes.effect: Life poured through a trained body — stand unbroken, healing as fast as you're hurt, rooted like the Quickwood itself. The warrior-warden's braid.
recipes.cannot: The overgrowth risk of Quickening plus the body's true limit — push too far and you grow WRONG; energy-hungry.
recipes.discovery: use — heal and endure together; a Flesh-Temple / Quickwood crossing

recipes.name: The True Word
recipes.effect: Attend a truth so fully and tell it so well that it BINDS — a story that, once spoken, cannot be unbelieved because it is simply true and simply told. The counter to the Cathedral of Convincing.
recipes.cannot: Only works on the actually-true; a beautiful lie breaks it; the Stillhold's buried truths resist being said.
recipes.discovery: use — reveal by attention + tell by story; the Wend keepers and the Numen both hold pieces

recipes.name: The Perfect Form
recipes.effect: A fight conducted like a proof — every strike lawful, every opening foreseen; the Marcher's duel as living structure.
recipes.cannot: Wildcraft unravels it; a truly chaotic foe breaks the proof.
recipes.discovery: taught at the Redline; or use — strike + bind in the forms

recipes.name: The Unraveling Blow
recipes.effect: A strike that lands where the plan broke — damage scales with the disorder you made first.
recipes.cannot: Against the truly formless there is no plan to break.
recipes.discovery: use — break a plan, then strike into it; Churn-taught

recipes.name: The Immovable Calm
recipes.effect: A guard that de-escalates — attacks are absorbed and somehow the fight is over; the Temple Guardian's signature.
recipes.cannot: Cannot pursue; the guard ends fights, doesn't win wars.
recipes.discovery: taught at the Flesh-Temple; or use — brace + de-escalate together

recipes.name: The Foregone Conclusion
recipes.effect: Out-model an opponent so completely their attack fails before it starts — the War-Thinker's checkmate.
recipes.cannot: The model is only as good as its inputs; wild luck defeats clean prediction.
recipes.discovery: Cogitarium-taught; or use — model then bind

recipes.name: The Grasping Green
recipes.effect: Roots bind the foe while living growth mends you mid-fight — attrition the green always wins. The Thorn-Warden's (and the Ent's) signature.
recipes.cannot: Needs earth; fire changes the equation entirely.
recipes.discovery: Deepwood Walkers teach it; or use — root-hold + heal in one fight

recipes.name: The Judging Beam
recipes.effect: A strike that reveals as it burns — illusions die first, then the target's cover. The Blaze-Lance signature.
recipes.cannot: What is truly as it appears takes only the burn.
recipes.discovery: Plateau-taught; or use — reveal + strike as one beam

recipes.name: The Quiet End
recipes.effect: A strike that concludes — against the dying, the raging, the unnatural, it simply finishes what was already ending. The Pale Reaper's mercy.
recipes.cannot: Cannot end what is whole and healthy — it concludes, never murders.
recipes.discovery: Ashwarden-taught, rarely; or use — ease + still in violence

recipes.name: The Hidden Road
recipes.effect: You step the dark from one place to another while a convincing trail walks somewhere else entirely. Pursuit follows the trail; you were never on it.
recipes.cannot: The decoy trail must end somewhere plausible. A pursuer who reaches its end and finds nothing knows exactly what you did.
recipes.discovery: use — shadowstep away in the same beat you lay a false trail; or taught in the Underlight

recipes.name: The Deep Quiet
recipes.effect: A silence so complete that violence loses its footing inside it — nobody can find the moment to begin.
recipes.cannot: The instant the quiet breaks, everything it was holding arrives at once, and all of it at you.
recipes.discovery: use — hold the breath while binding a room; or taught at the Stillhold

recipes.name: The Whole Body
recipes.effect: Body and motion become one instrument — you move through a fight, a fall, a crowd as though the world had agreed to make room.
recipes.cannot: It is still a body. It can still be outnumbered, and it still has to land.
recipes.discovery: use — perfect motion inside a somatic exertion; or trained at the Flesh-Temples

recipes.name: The Counted End
recipes.effect: You see an ending's exact hour and can bring it to that hour precisely — no sooner, no crueller, no waste. The Ashwarden mercy.
recipes.cannot: It cannot bring an ending that was not already coming. Used to hasten what should have lived, it marks the wielder — permanently, visibly.
recipes.discovery: use — read a death, then tend it in the same beat; or taught in the Hollow March

recipes.name: The Lattice-Engine
recipes.effect: Structure and mechanism braided: a system that not only runs but CANNOT run wrong — it corrects itself toward its design.
recipes.cannot: A self-correcting system corrects toward the design it was given. If the design is wrong, it will pursue that wrongness perfectly and forever.
recipes.discovery: use — bind a structure while mending its mechanism; taught wherever the Gearlands meet the Grand Lattice

recipes.name: The Sung Lattice
recipes.effect: Order given a voice: a structure sung into or out of true. The Harmonics' folk-craft reaching up toward what the Lattice-Cities do at scale.
recipes.cannot: The Valley's sung version is small. Against a true Lattice working, it is a folk song against a cathedral.
recipes.discovery: use — resonate against a bound structure; or shown by any Lattice-trained visitor to the Heights

recipes.name: The Grown Ward
recipes.effect: A boundary that is ALIVE — it grows into place, roots, and thickens over years. The Valley's oldest walls were made this way, and they are still growing.
recipes.cannot: It takes time to become anything. Planted today, it wards almost nothing; the Rootkin plant for grandchildren.
recipes.discovery: use — quicken a set boundary; or learned at Thornwake Glade

recipes.name: The Tended End
recipes.effect: Decay guided rather than merely allowed — a thing rots the right way, into the right soil, and feeds what comes next.
recipes.cannot: It is a slow craft, and it will not be hurried for your grief. Some things you want ended are ended usefully only over years.
recipes.discovery: use — pale-work something with a green hand; or taught where the Palelands meet the Quickwood

recipes.name: The Read Engine
recipes.effect: Machine-sense and mind-craft together: you do not merely read what a mechanism does — you reconstruct the MIND that designed it, and think its next thought.
recipes.cannot: Reconstructing a designer is not knowing them. The Cogitants warn that you are, in the end, reading yourself.
recipes.discovery: use — read a mechanism while holding it in a memory-palace; or in the Cogitarium's engine-halls

recipes.name: The Singing Dark
recipes.effect: See by sound where there is no light at all — the black is mapped by echo, and it is MORE legible than sight would make it.
recipes.cannot: A silent dark defeats it entirely. And the sound you make to see with, others can hear.
recipes.discovery: use — resonate in true darkness; a common Umbral trade-craft picked up by Harmonic travelers

recipes.name: The Burning Truth
recipes.effect: Revelation as a weapon: a truth spoken and LIT at once, that cannot be looked away from and cannot be denied by anyone present.
recipes.cannot: It burns what it lands on, including bystanders, including you. Both peoples who make it consider it a last resort; both use it more than they admit.
recipes.discovery: use — speak a true thing while kindling revelation; taught nowhere willingly

recipes.name: The Kind Lie
recipes.effect: A falsehood built entirely out of care — it holds someone up who would not survive the true thing today, and it holds only until they can.
recipes.cannot: It is still a lie, and it has a shelf life. When it fails — and it fails — the fall is longer for the height you gave them.
recipes.discovery: use — conceal a truth while tending the feeling under it; the Veilwrights teach it as an act of mercy and the Verists call that the greatest lie of all

recipes.name: The Cold Shadow
recipes.effect: Reasoning that hides itself: you build an argument, a plan, a case that is airtight and that nobody can find the shape of until it closes.
recipes.cannot: The argument still has to be sound. A hidden bad argument is simply a bad argument nobody caught yet — until they do.
recipes.discovery: use — conceal a chain of reasoning as you build it; a Cogitarium technique the Umbrals perfected

recipes.name: The Stopped Hour
recipes.effect: You become the fixed point a death cannot get past — not turning the hour aside but REFUSING it, and the refusal holds because you do not move.
recipes.cannot: You must not move. Not to eat, not to strike, not to save a second person. The hour waits exactly as long as you do.
recipes.discovery: use — anchor yourself while warding a death; there is no school that teaches this and several that forbid it

recipes.name: The Wild Green
recipes.effect: Growth let entirely off its leash — a wound closes wrong and strong, a wall goes green and comes apart, a field becomes a jungle in a season.
recipes.cannot: You cannot aim it and you cannot call it back. The Churnfolk think that is the point and the Valleyfolk think that is the problem.
recipes.discovery: use — quicken something without binding what it becomes; the Churn teaches it by accident, constantly

recipes.name: The Shrouded Wound
recipes.effect: Hold a mortal wound closed AND hide that you are hurt at all — you walk out of the room, and nobody knows to follow.
recipes.cannot: You are still holding a mortal wound closed. The shroud buys you distance, not time, and the moment you let either go, both fail.
recipes.discovery: use — staunch a wound while shrouding yourself in the same beat

recipes.name: The Measured Ruin
recipes.effect: Decay applied with a designer's precision — this beam and not that one, this hinge, this word in the contract. The building comes down exactly where you meant.
recipes.cannot: It requires you to be RIGHT about the structure. A measured ruin against a misread design brings down something you needed.
recipes.discovery: use — read a structure's order, then wither its load-bearing piece

recipes.name: The Open Mind
recipes.effect: You open a thin place INSIDE a memory-palace — a room in your own mind where the numinous can be met, held, and walked away from safely.
recipes.cannot: Safely is doing a great deal of work in that sentence. What you invite into your own mind has an address there now.
recipes.discovery: use — open a thin place while standing in the palace; the Numinous permit this only to their own, and the Cogitants do it anyway

recipes.name: The Harbored Truth
recipes.effect: Keep a true thing hidden and INTACT — unspoken, unweakened, unrotted — until the hour it can be spoken and land with its whole weight.
recipes.cannot: The truth keeps; the person waiting does not. Harboring a truth someone needed today in order to deploy it beautifully next year is a betrayal wearing patience.
recipes.discovery: use — harbor something and hold it true in one beat; the Verists debate endlessly whether this counts as honesty

recipes.name: The Bright Blade
recipes.effect: A struck light that is also a struck blade — the blow lands and REVEALS, and what it reveals is often why the blow was necessary.
recipes.cannot: Everyone sees what it reveals, including things you would rather not have shown, including about yourself.
recipes.discovery: use — kindle in the same motion as an edge-strike; common in the Redline's border wars

recipes.name: The Still Engine
recipes.effect: Command a mechanism to STOP and hold it stopped — a mill, a gate, a war-engine, a lock — bound in a peace it cannot argue with.
recipes.cannot: Machines do not resent being stopped, but their owners do. And a stopped engine is still an engine; someone will start it again.
recipes.discovery: use — bind an engine you have commanded; the Stillhold's quietest and least-loved export

recipes.name: The Borrowed Hour
recipes.effect: Refuse your own ending AND keep moving through it — a body that should be down is up, finishing what it came to do.
recipes.cannot: The hour is BORROWED. When you set it down, it arrives complete and all at once, and it does not negotiate. Somatics who do this twice do not do it a third time.
recipes.discovery: use — take a second wind while warding your own death; no tradition teaches it and every tradition knows someone who did it

recipes.name: The Named Chaos
recipes.effect: Name the order inside a chaos — not imposing structure but FINDING the one that was already there, and setting the thing into it.
recipes.cannot: There is not always one there. The Lattice's oldest error is naming an order onto a chaos that had none, and the chaos always eventually says so.
recipes.discovery: use — sense a chaos, then name its order in the same beat; the one craft both peoples claim to have invented
recipes.cultCondemns: The Grand Lattice's inner ring calls it a defect tolerated. Those who ride the Churn until nothing holds call it a cage with a nice view. **It is the only order anyone has ever been able to live inside.**
recipes.note: RECLASSIFIED 2026-07-12: Lattice [order] and Churnfolk [chaos] are ANTIPODES on the ring. This was mislabelled cross-axis. It is a BRAID — order that BREATHES.

recipes.name: The Long Memory
recipes.effect: A place's echo and a people's story braided into one telling — you recover not just what happened here but what it MEANT to those it happened to.
recipes.cannot: Meaning is not fact. What the story remembers and what the echo records will sometimes disagree, and you must choose which to carry.
recipes.discovery: use — read a place's echo while keeping its story; the Valley's finest folk-craft and it knows it

recipes.name: The Lit Lattice
recipes.effect: Written light bound into structure — a ward that is also a LAW: it does not merely block, it states the rule it is enforcing, and everyone can read it.
recipes.cannot: A stated rule can be argued with, lawyered, and found a gap in. A silent ward cannot. The Lattice considers the legibility worth it; the Umbrals find it hilarious.
recipes.discovery: use — bind a glimmer-script into a structure; standard at any Lattice border

recipes.name: The Unmade Seal
recipes.effect: A Precursor seal not picked but SCATTERED — its coherence unmade, so the lock stops being a lock rather than being opened.
recipes.cannot: What the seal was holding closed is now simply open, and nobody planned for that, including you. The Precursors sealed things for reasons.
recipes.discovery: use — scatter a seal you have addressed; the Churnfolk's favorite crime

recipes.name: The Beast-Read
recipes.effect: Read an animal as a body rather than a mystery — its fear, its intent, the half-second before it bolts or bites — and answer it in kind.
recipes.cannot: An animal read is not an animal tamed. Knowing exactly why it is about to bite you is not the same as it not biting you.
recipes.discovery: use — body-read a creature you are befriending

recipes.name: The Called-Back
recipes.effect: You call a thing back across the line it had already crossed. Not healing — RETURNING. The Ashwardens tend endings and the Rootkin tend growth, and they are antipodes with a working border and an exhausted mutual respect; to hold BOTH is to say *this ending was wrong, and I will carry the cost of unmaking it.* What comes back is alive. Whether it is the same is a question you will be answering for the rest of your life.
recipes.cannot: It does not come back UNCHANGED — it comes back as something that has been dead, and it will always have been dead. And it is YOURS now, permanently, in the way a promise is yours. The Rootkin consider this the near edge of their own taboo; the Ashwardens consider it a theft from the only honest thing there is. Neither people will teach it. **You cannot learn this. You can only DO it — and then discover what you did.**
recipes.discovery: use — palework and quickening held in the same act, at a moment when an ending is genuinely WRONG and you refuse it anyway. There is no teacher. There is no tome. **This is the cross-pole braid on the death↔life axis: the ONLY sanctioned road to your own antipode, and the hardest thing in the world to hold.**

recipes.name: The Warden's Blow
recipes.effect: You strike ONCE — decisively, completely, and in a way that makes every further strike unnecessary. The fight does not continue. It does not resume tomorrow. **The violence is spent, entire, in a single act, so that peace can hold behind it.** It is the most terrible mercy in the world.
recipes.cannot: You must actually END it. A Warden's Blow that leaves the thing standing is simply a murder attempt, and everyone will treat it as one. And you carry it — the Stillhold's priests will not bless it, will not absolve it, and will not pretend they do not know why you did it.
recipes.cultCondemns: The Silent (peace-cult) call this the greatest possible betrayal: peace bought with blood is not peace. The Redline's deepest cut calls it cowardice — a single blow to avoid the honest work of war. **They are both wrong, and the Stillhold's people are alive because someone did it.**
recipes.discovery: use — held together in a moment where holding only one end would have failed. No people teaches this; both peoples of the axis consider it, at best, a necessary shame.

recipes.name: The Truer Story
recipes.effect: A story that is **false in every fact and true in the only way that matters** — and it lands as truth, because it IS one. Not a lie that comforts. A shape that holds what the facts could not carry.
recipes.cannot: It must be TRUE in the way it claims to be. A Truer Story that is merely a kind lie collapses the moment it is tested, and takes the teller's credibility with it forever. And a Verist who knows what you did will say so, out loud, at the worst possible moment.
recipes.cultCondemns: The Flensing (truth-cult) call it the most dangerous thing in the world. The Last Mask (falsehood-cult) call it a failure of nerve — why stop at true? **It has saved more people than the truth has.**
recipes.discovery: use — held together in a moment where holding only one end would have failed. No people teaches this; both peoples of the axis consider it, at best, a necessary shame.

recipes.name: The Fallen Grace
recipes.effect: Mercy that has **felt the hunger** — and forgives anyway. Judgment that knows exactly what it is condemning, because it wanted the same thing. **It binds where clean judgment only breaks**, because the one being judged can feel that you understand.
recipes.cannot: You must actually have wanted it. A Fallen Grace offered by someone who never felt the pull is condescension, and it is heard as condescension, and it does nothing.
recipes.cultCondemns: The Unfallen (angelic-cult) call it corruption at the root. The Devouring (demonic-cult) call it weakness dressed as virtue. **It is the only judgment anyone has ever accepted.**
recipes.discovery: use — held together in a moment where holding only one end would have failed. No people teaches this; both peoples of the axis consider it, at best, a necessary shame.

recipes.name: The Unified Act
recipes.effect: Knowing and doing collapse into one motion. **There is no gap between the thought and the hand** — no tell, no wind-up, no moment where a reader could see it coming, because there is no 'coming'. It simply has happened.
recipes.cannot: It is one act. You cannot think your way through a second one while performing the first; the union is total and it is brief. And a body that has done this cannot immediately do it again.
recipes.cultCondemns: The Sculpted (body-cult) say the mind contaminates the body. The Discarnate (mind-cult) say the body contaminates the mind. **Both peoples' finest are, quietly, doing this.**
recipes.discovery: use — held together in a moment where holding only one end would have failed. No people teaches this; both peoples of the axis consider it, at best, a necessary shame.

recipes.name: The Load-Bearing Word
recipes.effect: A symbol you can **stand on**. Meaning made structural — a promise that holds weight, a name that stops a wall falling, an argument you can shelter under in the rain. **The abstract, made to bear load.**
recipes.cannot: It must be a meaning that is TRUE and a structure that is SOUND. Either half failing brings the whole thing down, and it brings it down on whoever was sheltering under it.
recipes.cultCondemns: The Leaden (concrete-cult) deny it can exist. The Untethered (abstract-cult) find it a vulgar waste of a good idea. **The Quiet House at the Crossing is built on one.**
recipes.discovery: use — held together in a moment where holding only one end would have failed. No people teaches this; both peoples of the axis consider it, at best, a necessary shame.

recipes.name: The Kept Appointment
recipes.effect: To be in the **right place at the right moment** — which is a far harder thing than being able to do either. You arrive exactly when arriving matters, and not one breath before or after.
recipes.cannot: You must know WHICH moment. The braid gets you there; it does not tell you where 'there' is. Arrive perfectly at the wrong appointment and you have wasted the rarest thing in the world.
recipes.cultCondemns: The Unlanded (space-cult) never arrive at all. The Stopped (time-cult) never leave. **Neither has ever kept an appointment.**
recipes.discovery: use — held together in a moment where holding only one end would have failed. No people teaches this; both peoples of the axis consider it, at best, a necessary shame.

recipes.name: The Cleared Ground
recipes.effect: The ending that **is** a founding. You unmake a thing so completely and so well that the ground it stood on is *ready* — and what you build there afterward stands on the ending as a foundation, not a scar.
recipes.cannot: It requires you to be RIGHT that the thing should end, and to actually build. A Cleared Ground with nothing raised on it is just a ruin with pretensions, and the Unmakers will tell you so.
recipes.cultCondemns: The Scouring (destruction-cult) clear and never build. The Ceaseless (creation-cult) build and never clear. **Every city worth living in was made this way.**
recipes.discovery: use — held together in a moment where holding only one end would have failed. No people teaches this; both peoples of the axis consider it, at best, a necessary shame.

recipes.name: The Harbored Flame
recipes.effect: A light you carry HIDDEN — warmth and revelation that the dark cannot put out and the Blaze cannot burn away, because it belongs to neither. **You can show one person one true thing in a place where showing anything would kill you both.**
recipes.cannot: It is ONE light, for ONE moment. It will not illuminate a room and it will not warm a camp. And carrying it means the Unlit want it out and the Blaze wants it BIGGER, and both will find you.
recipes.cultCondemns: The Unlit would put it out — it is a light, and lights must go. The Blaze would grow it until it consumed you — a light that is hidden is a light that is ASHAMED. **Neither can conceive of a small, sheltered, sufficient flame.**
recipes.discovery: use — held together at a moment when neither the dark nor the light alone could have saved it.

recipes.name: The Meaning-Engine
recipes.effect: A mechanism that **MEANS**. The Enginewrights build it and it runs; the Numinous make it matter; and the thing that results does work in the world that neither craft could do alone — a machine that is also a prayer, and functions as both.
recipes.cannot: It must actually MEAN something true. A Meaning-Engine built around a lie runs perfectly and produces nothing, and the failure is silent, and you will not notice for years.
recipes.cultCondemns: The Great Engine's tenders say meaning is an inefficiency to be optimized out. The Unbodied say a mechanism is a cage for a meaning. **Halvex Coil is what happens when only the first half is believed.**
recipes.discovery: use — a mechanism commanded and a thin place held open in the same act.

recipes.name: The Turning Word
recipes.effect: The argument that also **heals**. Reason alone cannot turn a heart; feeling alone cannot show it why. Held together, you can say the thing that is both TRUE and BEARABLE — and a person can actually change their mind, which is the rarest event in the world.
recipes.cannot: It must be both. A Turning Word that is warm and false is manipulation; one that is true and cold is just a Verist being cruel with extra steps. Get either half wrong and you have made an enemy who is also, now, correct about you.
recipes.cultCondemns: The Raw say reason is a wall built against feeling. The Bloodless say feeling is noise that corrupts a valid proof. **Neither has ever changed anyone's mind about anything.**
recipes.discovery: use — a true argument made in a moment where the other person could not have borne it plainly.

recipes.name: The Double Register
recipes.effect: You read a thing's STRUCTURE and its ENDING at once — where it bears load, and where it is already dying. **The two readings do not want to share a channel.** What you get is a map with two layers, slightly out of register: the load-bearing seam speaking one language and the death-register speaking another. **Rough. But readable — and the MISALIGNMENT is itself the information.** Where the two disagree, something is wrong with the thing in a way neither reading alone would find.
recipes.cannot: ⛔ **IT NEVER RESOLVES CLEANLY.** Stop trying to hold the two apart and you get noise; stop trying to hold them together and you get noise. **You must simply let both be present and accept the double vision.** It costs more than either reading alone, and it will always be rough.
recipes.discovery: use — deathsense and order-sense held open in the same act, on a thing that is both STRUCTURED and DYING. **Discovered in play by Silas Weir at the rune-binding, 2026-07-12** — he stopped fighting the friction and let the two readings sit out of register, and the runes landed where the spear already had an opinion about what it was.
recipes.authoredFrom: ⛔ EMERGENT — this recipe did not exist. It was created by a player co-activating two abilities the game had no combination for. The GM narrated it perfectly and the engine minted nothing. **Authored retroactively so the discovery is real.**

discoverability.byUse: Pairing the two parts in a single beat surfaces the braid as a DISCOVERY — logged to codex + narrativeLog, thereafter a known technique. This is the primary path: players FIND combinations by trying things, and the world remembers they found them.
discoverability.byReading: A rune-cast or True Attention on a scene/self can hint a LATENT braid available to the wielder ('these two of yours want to be one') — discovery via the read.
discoverability.byTeaching: Masters know braids; a teacher (named in each recipe's discovery) can grant one directly, at a price or a bond.
discoverability.generative: SNG-020 can author a NEW braid in-grain when a player creatively pairs two owned abilities no authored recipe covers — validated, persisted, added to the discoverable set. The recipe list GROWS through play.
discoverability.engineTie: Recipes carry `parts` (the two ability ids), `functions`, `domains`, `discovery`. The engine: on a two-ability beat, check for a matching recipe (authored or generate one); gate on owning both; surface + persist the discovery. Extends emergence-recipes (SNG-010). Flagged to SNG-026.

combinationTiers.within-tradition: Two crafts of the SAME people. The most learnable — their own masters teach these.
combinationTiers.kin-civilization: Two peoples who share a secondary disposition lean and therefore a border (world_framing: adjacency = kinship). Learnable where their lands meet.
combinationTiers.cross-axis: Two peoples from entirely different axes. Rare, powerful, strange — usually discovered by USE, in a moment where someone needed both at once. Few are taught anywhere.
combinationTiers.cross-pole: The two poles of a SINGLE axis — the braids that belong to neither people and require standing in both (harbored_flame, meaning_engine, the_turning_word). The hardest and the most meaningful: to hold an axis whole rather than choose an end of it.

crossPoleNote: The cross-pole braids are the ONLY sanctioned road to your own antipode — the one thing the domain-access model forbids by every ordinary means. Four now exist, one per built axis pair: the_harbored_flame (dark↔light) · the_meaning_engine (mechanical↔spiritual) · the_turning_word (emotional↔logical) · **the_called_back (death↔life — added 2026-07-12 because Erik's necromancer RAISED A RAVEN and the braid he had already played did not exist).** To carry a braid is to hold an axis WHOLE rather than choose an end of it. It is the rarest and most meaningful thing a character can do, and no people will teach it.

theTwelveBraids.principle: ONE braid per axis. Twelve axes, twelve braids. To carry one is to hold an axis WHOLE rather than choose an end of it — and it is the ONLY sanctioned road to your own antipode, which the domain-access model closes to you forever by every ordinary means.
theTwelveBraids.cultsCondemnAllOfThem: ⛔ THE CULT OF EACH PURE POLE CALLS ITS AXIS'S BRAID AN ABOMINATION — and both cults of an axis condemn the SAME braid, for opposite reasons. The living civilizations quietly permit them. **That disagreement IS the difference between a people and a cult.**
